directory.state: VERIFICATION STATUS: 'Verified' = URL confirmed via direct web search Feb 2026. 'Cross-ref' = URL cross-referenced against AOA 2023 compilation and/or CMS State Plan Amendments. 'Best known' = URL based on training knowledge; should be independently verified.
1. URLs change frequently as states redesign their websites. Always verify links are current before relying on them.
2. Many states require acceptance of CPT (AMA) and CDT (ADA) license agreements before accessing fee schedule data.
3. Some states (PA, CT, NH) require portal login or provider credentials for full fee schedule downloads.
4. Nearly all states use RBRVS-based methodologies for physician services, but conversion factors vary significantly.
5. In managed care states, FFS fee schedules typically serve as a floor or reference rate. MCO contracted rates may differ.
6. This directory focuses on physician/professional fee schedules. Most states also publish separate schedules for hospital outpatient, DME, lab, dental, pharmacy.
7. The CMS Medicaid access rule (CMS-2442-F1) requires states to publish FFS rates in machine-readable format by July 1, 2026.
